mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack reduces corporate profit margins and consumer spending growth, driving negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales signals robust aggregate demand that lifts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS yields negative forward returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity constraints and supply bottlenecks that trigger short-term market corrections within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines produce positive 5-day returns in >65% of late-cycle observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate surpasses 4.0 percent, borrowing costs for corporations and households rise sharply, suppressing equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising unemployment and falling total nonfarm payrolls confirms a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer income and drives negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields positive forward returns in >55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect increased construction activity and durable goods demand, which directly boosts corporate revenue forecasts and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 or forward returns are negative in >60% of high HOUST periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals rapid capacity utilization that fuels short-term momentum and positive equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases produce negative 5-day returns in >60% of post-shock recovery observations.
